items.title: Apple Intelligence approved for launch in China with Alibaba and Baidu
items.content: The deal, which was rumored to be in the works last year, marks an important step for Apple's AI ambitions in a key market.

items.title: Apple bans home services from its upcoming Maps ads
items.content: Apple has published the policies governing its upcoming Maps advertising business, revealing a strategy that differs from Google’s. The new rules prohibit home services busine

items.title: India bets billions on breaking China’s grip on smartphone manufacturing
items.content: New Delhi announced a $6.5 billion smartphone manufacturing program and a $13.3 billion semiconductor push to deepen India's electronics supply chain.

items.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move

items.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
items.content: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

items.title: Microsoft patches bug in video game Age of Empires II
items.content: The vulnerability in the decades-old game could have allowed hackers to take over victims’ computers with a malicious game invite.

items.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
items.content: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI lab

items.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
items.content: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

items.title: Maker of Drone Boats Used in Iran War to Open New Texas Shipyard
items.content: Saronic Technologies will make naval and commercial vessels at the facility. The company’s naval drone helped rescue the crew of a downed Apache helicopter last month.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz Tanker Traffic Erodes Further as Oil Prices Rise
items.content: Very few ships passed through the waterway on the first full day of the U.S. naval blockade of Iran.

items.title: Anthropic Inches Toward a Mega-I.P.O.
items.content: The artificial intelligence lab is said to have taken more steps that are consistent with a company aiming to go public in the fall.

items.title: Google Ordered to Give A.I. Rivals More Access on Android Smartphones
items.content: The decision by European Union regulators is a response to fears that Google will use its vast Android user base to gain an edge in A.I.
